behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "signature does not match its body...401...not forwarded." Slot B: handoff boundary (HTTP handler -> signature verifier -> queue adapter). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a pure verifier test can pass while handler wiring still forwards on mismatch. Rejection gate: no text requiring real network/OS/subprocess, so not System.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with real middleware/module wiring and a fake queue sink. Send a signed request with an intentionally wrong HMAC over the raw body; assert HTTP 401 and assert queue sink receives zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "written to the persistent queue before...200 acknowledgment." Slot B: handoff/order across modules (handler -> queue persistence -> response writer). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can miss real ordering bugs where response is sent before persistence promise resolves. Rejection gate: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess; local persistence wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use integrated handler + real queue module against a local test persistence backend. Instrument queue write completion and response emission timestamps/order; assert write commit occurs before 200 is sent.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing...to finish." Slot B: async handoff boundary (enqueue/dispatch vs response lifecycle). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a mocked function test can miss coupling where response path accidentally awaits worker completion. Rejection gate: no requirement for real OS/network timing beyond module-level async control.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with a downstream processor stub that blocks on a controllable promise. Send a valid event, assert response is returned before the promise is released, then release and verify processing eventually completes.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "within 3 seconds wall-clock...slow and concurrent." Slot B: real-infra signal (event loop scheduling, real HTTP concurrency, wall-clock behavior). Slot C: if lowered to Integration with faked timers/in-process stubs, scheduler/socket contention regressions can be hidden. Rejection gate: no model-output dependency, so not Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Start the actual Node service process and issue concurrent real HTTP requests while earlier events are intentionally slow in downstream workers. Measure end-to-end client-observed latency; assert each new request is acknowledged under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "ID was already received...not written...a second time." Slot B: handoff/state boundary (dedupe store/index + queue write path). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, dedupe logic may pass while persistence uniqueness or repository wiring still permits duplicates. Rejection gate: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess; local persistence integration is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with the real dedupe/persistence modules. Submit the same event ID twice and assert exactly one persisted queue record/enqueue effect.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "queue reports it is full...returns 503 with Retry-After." Slot B: module-boundary error mapping (queue error contract -> HTTP translation). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can miss mismatched error types/codes across module boundary that prevent correct 503 mapping. Rejection gate: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency is stated; no model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate handler with queue adapter that emits the real 'full' error shape. Send a valid request and assert status 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit." Slot B: boundary is local persistence implementation + configuration interaction (not external network/OS primitive). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, mocked storage can always 'accept' writes and hide real limit-enforcement bugs. Rejection gate: text specifies local persistent queue behavior, not subprocess/real external API, so System is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real queue implementation with a small configured capacity on a local test backend. Fill to limit, attempt one additional enqueue, and assert a deterministic capacity-rejection error and unchanged persisted count.
